description: EmDash-specific extension data embedded inside a release record under its `extensions` field. Carries the trust contract (declaredAccess) the sandbox enforces. EXPERIMENTAL: shape may change before stabilisation.

main.description: Typed embedded object carrying the EmDash plugin's structured access manifest. Lives under the release record's `extensions` map keyed by this NSID. The `$type` field is required when embedded; clients use it to dispatch to this schema.

declaredAccess.description: Structured per-category access manifest. The sandbox enforces every operation declared here at runtime.

Map of access category to a per-category block of named operations. Categories not enumerated here cannot be declared today; clients MUST reject release records that include unrecognised top-level fields. Within a known category, an unrecognised operation key is also a hard reject. Within an operation's constraint object, unrecognised keys are advisory and surfaced verbatim in install-consent UI. Note that the bundled manifest may use boolean `true` as sugar for an empty constraint object; on-PDS records canonicalise this to `{}` because Lexicon validation does not accept boolean values for typed object fields.

content.description: Access to site content (posts, pages, custom collections).

email.description: Sending mail through the host's mail service, and participating in its delivery pipeline.

contentAccess.description: Operations under content. Each operation value is a constraint object; an empty object grants the operation with no constraints applied.

write.description: Plugin may create, update, or delete content records. Implies `read`.

contentReadConstraints.description: Constraint object for content.read. No constraint keys are normatively defined here; the object is open to additional unrecognised keys that future runtimes may interpret.

contentWriteConstraints.description: Constraint object for content.write. No constraint keys are normatively defined here; the object is open to additional unrecognised keys that future runtimes may interpret.

read.description: Plugin may read media metadata and fetch media bytes.

write.description: Plugin may upload, modify, or delete media. Implies `read`.

mediaReadConstraints.description: Constraint object for media.read. No constraint keys are normatively defined here.

mediaWriteConstraints.description: Constraint object for media.write. No constraint keys are normatively defined here.

request.description: Plugin may make outbound HTTP requests. Constraints scope the access; an empty object grants unrestricted requests.

networkRequestConstraints.description: Constraint object for network.request. Open shape — additional constraint keys (rate limits, etc.) may be declared and will be surfaced advisorily until normatively defined by a follow-on RFC.

allowedHosts.description: Allow-list of outbound host patterns. Each entry is a hostname pattern with no scheme, path, or port; a leading '*.' wildcard is permitted for subdomains. Field absent means no host restriction; an empty array MUST NOT appear in records.

events.description: Plugin observes and may mutate every outgoing message (before and/or after send), including mail from the host and other plugins.

transport.description: Plugin becomes the host's mail transport; every message the site sends is delivered through it. Exclusive: installing replaces the current transport.

emailSendConstraints.description: Constraint object for email.send. No constraint keys are normatively defined here; vocabulary for rate limits and recipient allow-lists is reserved for a follow-on RFC.

emailEventsConstraints.description: Constraint object for email.events. No constraint keys are normatively defined here; phase scoping (beforeSend/afterSend) is reserved for a follow-on RFC and surfaced advisorily until then.

emailTransportConstraints.description: Constraint object for email.transport. No constraint keys are normatively defined here. Exclusivity is a property of the host extension point: clients treat email.transport as exclusive regardless of record contents.

fragments.description: Plugin injects script and/or style fragments into rendered pages.

pageFragmentsConstraints.description: Constraint object for page.fragments. No constraint keys are normatively defined here; position/placement scoping is reserved for a follow-on RFC.

read.description: Plugin may read site user records.

usersReadConstraints.description: Constraint object for users.read. No constraint keys are normatively defined here.
